---
title: "Portfolio"
author: "Mare van Oevelen"
format: dashboard
orientation: columns
editor: visual
---

# Background

Dua Lipa vs Billie eilish. Both artists in pop genre, but have different sounds. Both have 3 studio albums which are used in this project. Analysis will be done about all albums, but also some specific songs for more in depth insights.

# Track-level features

## Column

What insights into your corpus can be drawn from Spotify's track-level features (valence, danceability, etc.)?

## ###Row

![](images/combi2_energy.1.png)

![](images/combi2_valence.1.png)

![](images/combi2_danceability.1.png)

![](images/combi2_energy+valence.1.png)

# Chroma features

## Column {width="40%"}

These two chromagrams show the chroma information about two Dua Lipa songs: Be The One and Don't Start Now. More information in progress.

Also two more chromagrams coming about Billie Eilish: Your Power and Oxytocin.

## ###Row

![](images/chromagram_bto.1.png)

![](images/chromagram_dsn.1.png)

## \### Row {height="20%"}

# Timbre features

What insights can be drawn from timbre features? (self similarity matrices)

# Loudness

What insights can be drawn from 'loudness' (power) features?

# Temporal features

What is notable, effective, or ineffective about structural segments, meter, rhythm, or beats in your corpus, as derived from Spotify features?

# Classification/regression

Where explicit labels in your corpus exist, how strong are spotify features for classification/regression?

# Clustering

## Column

This is a dendrogram that contains the full discography of Billie Eilish and Dua Lipa. Both discographies are in the dataset and used in the dendrogram. On the x-axis are the names of the songs, which are in random order. On the y-axis the difference between the songs are shown.

As we can see there are a lot of tiny branches connecting two songs, which means many songs are considered to be alike. As expected, there are also some bigger branches, which means that there are also songs that are not alike at all. Both artists make music that have a completely different sound, so it makes a lot of sense that the dendrogram shows that they are not very similar.

## ###Row

![](dendrogram_duabillie_4.png)

# Contribution

What have you learned from this set of analyses? Who could these conclusions benefit and how?
